fields.description:
## Finding

Canonical contributor guidance hardcodes a specific maintainer/bot DCO identity even though project contribution policy requires contributors to sign with their own real configured identity.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:124-169` documents the Gerrit workflow and currently prescribes `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com>`.
- `.agents/rules/rebar.md:28-34` repeats the same personal bot identity.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:198-214` requires a real contributor name and email for DCO sign-off.

## Remediation

Make the canonical guidance identity-neutral: contributors and agents use the real name and email configured for the actor performing the commit (`git config user.name` / `git config user.email`). Retain Joe Oakhart's bot identity only in narrowly scoped automation guidance for a workflow that actually runs as that bot.

Cascade flag: documentation-only contract change; verify all imported/copied agent guidance (`AGENTS.md`, `.agents/rules/*.md`, and any other contributor-facing guidance under `docs/`) so contradictory instructions do not remain. Automation-owned paths (`infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/fixtures/`) and experiment corpora (`docs/experiments/`) legitimately reference the bot identity and are explicitly out of scope for the sweep and for the consistency check below.

Add a deterministic consistency check: a `make lint`-integrated grep step (e.g. a target in the `Makefile`, or a script under `scripts/` invoked from `make lint`) that runs
`git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/**/*.md'`
(explicitly excluding `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/`, and `docs/experiments/`) and fails the build if it finds any match. Document, next to the check, which file owns it and that `infra/`/`.github/workflows/`/`tests/`/`docs/experiments/` are the intentional exclusions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AGENTS.md` tells contributors and agents to verify `git config user.name` and `git config user.email` and to sign off with that configured real identity. Verify: `grep -q 'git config user.name' AGENTS.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' AGENTS.md`.
- [ ] `.agents/rules/rebar.md` and any derived guidance no longer instruct every actor to use Joe Oakhart's bot identity. Verify: `! git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- '.agents/rules/*.md' AGENTS.md`.
- [ ] Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it (e.g. `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `CLAUDE.local.md`) and is not presented as general contributor guidance. Verify: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart'` returns matches ONLY under `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/`, or `docs/experiments/`; any match outside those prefixes fails the check.
- [ ] `CONTRIBUTING.md`, canonical agent guidance, and generated/imported guidance describe one consistent DCO contract, defined concretely as: all three name "the committer's own configured git identity (`git config user.name`/`user.email`)" as the sign-off source, and none of them names a specific third party (Joe Oakhart or otherwise) as the required sign-off for general contributions. Verify: `grep -c "committer's own configured git identity\|configured git identity" CONTRIBUTING.md AGENTS.md .agents/rules/rebar.md` reports at least one match in each of the three files, AND `git grep -l 'Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- CONTRIBUTING.md AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md'` returns no results.
- [ ] A deterministic consistency check exists and fails the build on a reintroduced hardcoded personal sign-off: implemented as a `make lint`-integrated grep step (script or Makefile target) scanning `AGENTS.md`, `.agents/rules/*.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `docs/**/*.md` (recursive, so it also covers `docs/adr/`, `docs/design/`, `docs/migrations/`, `docs/calibration/`, etc.) for the literal strings `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` and `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart`, excluding `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/`, and `docs/experiments/`. Verify: the new check's script/target exists, running it against the post-change guidance files exits 0, and running it against a scratch copy with the hardcoded string reinjected exits non-zero.
